zh.Hexham, Northumberland, England: 英國諾圖波藍德可哈姆

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Graham於1242年出生在英國諾圖波藍德時，她的父親Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith36歲，母親Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith32歲。她於1259年與Adam de Swynburne在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德勒頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1284年在她的家鄉享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Graham于1242年出生在英国諾圖波藍德时，她的父亲Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith36岁，母亲Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith32岁。她于1259年与Adam de Swynburne在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德勒頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1284年在她的家乡享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Graham was born in 1242, in Northumberland, England, her father, Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith, was 36 and her mother, Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith, was 32. She married Adam de Swynburne in 1259, in Chollerton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1284, in her hometown, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Graham est née en 1242, à Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Adam de Swynburne en 1259, à Chollerton, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1284, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Graham 1242 in Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith 36 und ihre Mutter Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith 32. Sie hat 1259 in Chollerton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom Adam de Swynburne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1284 im Alter von 42 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Graham è nata il nel 1242 nel Northumberland, England, sua padre, Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Adam de Swynburne nel 1259, nel Chollerton, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1284, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Grahamが1242年にイギリスノースウンバーランドで生まれた時，父Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeithは36歳，母Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeithは32歳であった。1259年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドチオラートンでAdam de Swynburneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1284年に故郷で42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Graham(이)가 1242년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1259년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 컬레르턴에서 Adam de Swynburne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1284년 그녀의 고향에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Graham nasceu em 1242, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Adam de Swynburne em 1259, em Chollerton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1284, em sua cidade natal, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Graham родилась в 1242 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam de Swynburne в 1259 г. в местности чоллиртон, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1284 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Graham nació en 1242, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Adam de Swynburne en 1259, en Chollerton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1284, en su pueblo, a la edad de 42 años.
